meta.title: Training Needs Analysis — Role × Competence
meta.subtitle: Scaffolding crew, North-tower project
meta.author: HSE Operations
meta.deid_notice: Competence and appraisal data is personal data. The matrix circulates by ROLE LABEL, not personal name. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; where one named person is the sole holder of a competence the gap is reported, not the identity. The re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This Training Needs Analysis baselines the role×competence profile for the named roles in scope (ISO 45001 §7.2 competence, §7.3 awareness), scoring each cell current-vs-required on the 4-level scale (aware/trained/competent/expert) from a named evidence source. It flags single-points-of-failure, tracks certification expiry, and prioritises a costed training plan by risk × legal mandate × gap size. Decision-support only; a competent person must review the output.

sections.caption: Current level vs required level; cell = current (gap). Banded on KB-DATA-COMPETENCE-LEVELS from training records + appraisal evidence.

rows.title: Scaffolding Supervisor below statutory scaffold-inspection competence
rows.description: Required level 3 (competent person for scaffold inspection) is unmet (current level 2). Statutory competence requirement — cited to the jurisdiction's legal-required-competency duty; never downgraded to pass.
rows.evidence: Training record TR-114; legal-required-competency map (Q5 row)

rows.title: Single inspection-competent scaffolder on nights
rows.description: Only one named person holds level-3 scaffold-inspection competence on the night shift — a single-point-of-failure; if absent, no compliant inspection. Reported by role, not identity.

rows.title: Labourers lack work-at-height rescue awareness
rows.description: Required level 2 for rescue assist is unmet (current level 1).

rows: Cross-train a second night-shift scaffolder to L3
sections.caption: Competences held by only one person (reported by role, not identity — small-cell suppression).
Time-limited certificates with refresher lead-time; an expired statutory certificate is high-priority.

sections.text: Prioritised training plan (SMART, owners, dates, cost)

rows.action: Enrol Scaffolding Supervisor on accredited scaffold-inspection competent-person course; assess to L3
Renew the two work-at-height/IPAF certificates (overdue) before next access work
Cross-train a second night-shift scaffolder to L3 inspection (resolve the SPOF)
Deliver work-at-height rescue-assist training to the two labourers (L1→L2)

sections.text: Training in the hierarchy of controls

Training is an administrative control (KB-SNIP-HOC). It is paired with the higher-order site controls (edge protection, exclusion zones, a managed inspection regime) — it is never offered as the sole control of the fall-from-height hazard.

Indicative course costs are [ASSUMPTION] pending supplier quotes. Where a current competence level had no evidence source it is flagged [GAP] and treated as unmet until evidenced — not assumed competent.

This TNA is decision-support and must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person (the Competence & Training Manager SME persona ran a pre-output review; it precedes and does not replace the human competent-person sign-off). Next review: on workforce change or annually.

items: Jurisdiction legal-required-competency duty (resolved at Q5; India → hse-india, no national form number)
